query: SK Hynix ADR Nasdaq listing SKHY first trading date July 2026 latest news, pricing date, roadshow status, SEC review as of late June 2026. Is July 10 still the expected trading date? Any reports of delay or schedule slippage?
answer:
## Summary

**As of the latest available reporting (July 2, 2026), July 10, 2026 remains the expected Nasdaq debut date for SK Hynix's ADRs (ticker SKHY), and no delay or schedule slippage has been reported.**

Key findings:

- **Status:** The listing process was described as being in its "final stage." SK Hynix filed an **amended registration statement (Form F-1/A) with the SEC on June 30, 2026** — a normal step toward effectiveness that added detail on use of proceeds and investor accessibility. An F-6 (ADR registration form) was also filed. Confidential DRS/DRS-A filings preceded the public F-1.

- **Timeline (per June 24 board resolution):** Nasdaq trading debut **July 10, 2026**; subscription/payment July 14; new-share KRX listing July 29.

- **Deal terms:** Up to **17.79M new shares** (~2.5% of outstanding), targeting up to **KRW 45.45T (~$29.4–29.65B)** — potentially the largest ADR offering ever. Indicative price ~**$165–166/ADR**. Underwriters: BofA, Citigroup, Goldman Sachs, J.P. Morgan.

- **Roadshow/pricing:** No explicit roadshow dates published; final pricing to be set with underwriters based on market conditions.

- **Important caveats:** SK Hynix and media consistently stress the dates are **"tentative and subject to change,"** pending SEC effectiveness and market conditions. As of the data cutoff, **no news confirmed the registration was declared SEC-effective or that final pricing had occurred** — events that would normally happen just before a July 10 debut. Substantive reporting clusters around June 24–July 2, 2026; I could not retrieve a definitive July 3–4 "effective/priced" confirmation. So July 10 should be read as "still on track as last reported," not fully locked-in.

Full details, sources, and structured data points have been provided via the StructuredOutput tool.

sources.snippet: Filed with SEC to raise ~$29.65 billion; trading expected to begin July 10; up to 17.79 million new shares; each common share represented by ten ADRs.

sources.snippet: Ticker SKHY on Nasdaq Global Select Market; 17.79M shares (~2.5%); underwriters BofA Securities, Citigroup, Goldman Sachs, J.P. Morgan; proposed ADR price ~$166.

sources.snippet: Tentative listing date July 10, 2026; target 45.45 trillion won; proceeds for capacity expansion including Yongin cluster.

sources.snippet: SK hynix will list ADRs on Nasdaq on July 10; opted to issue new shares rather than convert treasury stock; board approved June 24.

sources.snippet: Board resolution June 24, 2026; scheduled listing date July 10, 2026; subscription/payment July 14; new-share KRX listing July 29.

sources.snippet: Board approved; up to 45.45 trillion won; at top of range would be largest ADR offering ever, surpassing Alibaba's $21.8B 2014 debut.

sources.title: SK Hynix U.S. stock listing ADR debut August 2026 (Reuters)
sources.snippet: Reuters (June 10) reported SEC clearance could arrive the week of June 22 with a listing as early as August; superseded by later July 10 target.

sources.snippet: Indicated $165.26 per ADR; ticker SKHY; trading after July 10.

sources.snippet: Amended F-1 registration statement (could not be fetched directly; corroborated via secondary reporting as filed June 30, 2026).

key_facts: July 10, 2026 remains the expected/scheduled Nasdaq trading debut date for SK Hynix ADRs (ticker SKHY) as of the latest reporting (July 2, 2026).
No reports of delay, postponement, or schedule slippage were found; the process was described as being in its 'final stage.'
SK Hynix filed an amended registration statement (Form F-1/A) with the SEC on June 30, 2026, indicating SEC review is progressing toward effectiveness.
SK Hynix's board formally approved the ADR issuance on June 24, 2026.
Both SK Hynix and media consistently note the dates are 'tentative and subject to change,' pending SEC effectiveness and market conditions.
Offering: up to 17.79 million new common shares (~2.5% of shares outstanding), targeting up to KRW 45.45 trillion (~$29.4-29.65 billion) — potentially the largest ADR offering ever.
Underwriters: BofA Securities, Citigroup, Goldman Sachs, and J.P. Morgan.
Indicative ADR price ~$165.26-$166 (not yet final; to be priced with underwriters).
Scheduled subscription/payment date July 14, 2026; new-share KRX listing July 29, 2026.
Proceeds earmarked for memory capacity expansion: EUV equipment (~$7.7B), Yongin/Y1 fab, and P&T7 Cheongju advanced packaging.
As of the data cutoff, no news confirmed the registration was declared SEC-effective or that final pricing had occurred.

data_points.metric: Offering as share of outstanding shares
